questions.text: An aqueous solution of [Ti(H2O)6]3+ ion has a mild violet colour of low intensity. Which of the following statements is incorrect?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: The ion absorbs visible light in the region of ~ 5000 Å
options.explanation: This statement is correct because the color of the ion is violet, which is in the visible light spectrum. The wavelength of violet light is approximately 5000 Å.

options.text: The colour results from an electronic transition of one electron from the t2g to an eg orbital
options.explanation: This statement is also correct because the titanium atom in the [Ti(H2O)6]3+ ion has a 3d1 electron configuration. The water molecules are weak-field ligands, so they do not cause much splitting of the d orbitals. This means that the energy difference between the t2g and eg orbitals is small, and it takes very little energy to excite an electron from the t2g to the eg orbital.

options.text: The low colour-intensity is because of a low probability of transition
options.explanation: This statement is also correct because the energy difference between the t2g and eg orbitals is small, so the transition does not occur very often. This is why the color of the ion is not very intense.

options.text: The transition is the result of metal-ligand back bonding
options.explanation: [Ti(H2O)6]3+ has one unpaired electron in its d-subshell which gives rise to the d-d transition to impart colour. Whenever light falls on the transition element compounds electrons excite and electrons absorb energy and excite. When these electrons de-excite they release visible light wavelength. That's why transition element compounds exhibit colour.

questions.explanation: [Ti(H2O)6]3+ has one unpaired electron in its d-subshell which gives rise to the d-d transition to impart colour. Whenever light falls on the transition element compounds electrons excite and electrons absorb energy and excite. When these electrons de-excite they release visible light wavelength. That's why transition element compounds exhibit colour.

questions.text: Which has a complex anion?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Na3AlF6 is not a complex anion because the aluminum atom is surrounded by six fluoride ligands, but these ligands are shared between three aluminum atoms. Therefore, it is not a complex anion.
K2SiF6 is not a complex anion because the silicon atom is surrounded by six fluoride ligands, but these ligands are also shared between two silicon atoms. Therefore, it is not a complex anion.

A complex anion is an anion that contains a metal atom surrounded by ligands. Na3AlF6 and K2SiF6 actually represent double salts since the presence of F– is detected as 3NaF.AlF3 and 2KF.SiF4. The true complex is K2PtCl6.
All is not an option because only K2PtCl6 has a complex anion. The other two compounds, Na3AlF6 and K2SiF6, do not have complex anions.

questions.explanation: A complex anion is an anion that contains a metal atom surrounded by ligands. Na3AlF6 and K2SiF6 actually represent double salts since the presence of F– is detected as 3NaF.AlF3 and 2KF.SiF4. The true complex is K2PtCl6.

questions.text: Which of the following is a typical transition metal?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Group No. IIIB (Sc, Y, La) are non-typical transition elements.
Group No. IIIB (Sc, Y, La) are non-typical transition elements.
Group No. IIB (Zn, Cd, H) are non-typical transition elements.

Typical transition elements show general properties (colour, paramagnetism, alloy formation etc.) of transition elements while non-typical transition element don't show general properties of transition elements. Group No. IIIB (Sc, Y, La) and IIB (Zn, Cd, H) are non-typical transition elements while group No. IVB, VB, VIB, VIIB, VIIIB and are typical transition elements. So Co shows typical behaviour.

questions.explanation: Typical transition elements show general properties (colour, paramagnetism, alloy formation etc.) of transition elements while non-typical transition element don't show general properties of transition elements. Group No. IIIB (Sc, Y, La) and IIB (Zn, Cd, H) are non-typical transition elements while group No. IVB, VB, VIB, VIIB, VIIIB and are typical transition elements.

questions.text: The central transition metal atom along with ligands is called:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: A complex ion refers to an ion formed when a coordination complex carries a net electric charge. This happens when the central metal ion in the complex gains or loses electrons, leading to a charged species. Complex ions can have various ligands coordinated to the central metal ion.

A ligand is a molecule, ion, or atom that donates a pair of electrons to the central metal ion, forming coordinate covalent bonds. Ligands have lone pairs of electrons that can bond with the metal ion, and they play a crucial role in stabilizing coordination complexes.

The coordination sphere, also known as the coordination complex or coordination entity, refers to the central metal ion and all the ligands surrounding it. It includes both the central metal ion and the ligands coordinated to it, forming a stable complex.

The term "complex compound" is a general term used to describe coordination complexes. A complex compound is a chemical compound formed by the coordination of ligands to a central metal ion through coordinate covalent bonds.

questions.explanation: The correct option for the given question is C) Coordination sphere. The coordination sphere refers to the central transition metal ion along with all the ligands that are coordinated to it, forming a coordination complex.

Ligand is a molecule or ion that can donate a pair of electrons to form a coordinated covalent bond with a metal ion. Hence, this option is incorrect.
A complex compound is a compound that contains one or more complex ions. Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Coordinate sphere is the term used to refer to the central atom and all of the ligands that are bonded to it. questions.text: Which of the following elements can form interstitial alloy with transition elements?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Zinc is too large to fit into the interstices of transition metals. It would instead form substitutional alloys, where it would replace some of the atoms of the transition metal in the crystal lattice.

Magnesium is too large to fit into the interstices of transition metals. It would instead form substitutional alloys, where it would replace some of the atoms of the transition metal in the crystal lattice.
Hydrogen is the only element that is small enough to fit into the interstices of transition metals. It does this by forming a covalent bond with the transition metal atom.

questions.explanation: Hydrogen is the only element that is small enough to fit into the interstices of transition metals. It does this by forming a covalent bond with the transition metal atom.
